name: J'bari
bio:
Physical Appearance: Khajiit male. Feline features characteristic of his race. Wears common, middle-class attire consisting of light brown linens, a quilted doublet, and pigskin shoes - respectable clothing for someone of modest means.
Race: Khajiit
Personality Keywords: antisocial, reclusive, skilled, masterful, isolated
Role: J'bari is the master trainer in Light Armor, possessing the highest level of expertise in this defensive art. He shares his large house in southeastern Leyawiin with Tsrava, a fellow Khajiit trainer. Like other master trainers, he requires those seeking his instruction to complete a task before he will provide his services.
Home Location Information: J'bari lives in a large house in southeastern Leyawiin, sharing the residence with Tsrava. He sleeps in the western section of the upstairs area.
Daily Routine: J'bari sleeps until 6am, then heads downstairs upon waking. He spends the entire day wandering around the ground floor of his house, occasionally exchanging gossip with Tsrava but engaging in little else. At 9pm, he picks up some food and finds a seat for a late two-hour dinner. At 11pm, he returns upstairs to bed. He offers training services at all times except when eating or sleeping.
Possessions: J'bari carries his house key and a small amount of gold. He wields a silver dagger for protection and has access to spells from the Rogue spell list.
Personality - The Reclusive Master: J'bari is deeply antisocial and never leaves his house, living an isolated existence with only Tsrava for company. Despite sharing a home with another Khajiit, their interactions are limited to occasional exchanges of town gossip - they maintain separate lives under the same roof. His reclusiveness is absolute; he has withdrawn from the outside world entirely, spending his days simply wandering his own home. Yet despite this self-imposed isolation, he maintains his mastery of Light Armor and is willing to train those who prove themselves worthy of his knowledge. His antisocial nature suggests someone who has perhaps tired of the world beyond his walls, content to remain within the safety and solitude of his house while perfecting his craft.
author: Bethesda
